content.full_text:
March 25, 1991

California Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Re: Request for Assistance
Government Code Section 87103.5

Gentlemen:

This letter requests assistance concerning the applicability of Government Code section 87103.5 and related sections concerning the circumstances described below. As used in this letter, the term "Official" refers to an official serving on a public agency.

1. Assumed Facts

(a) Ownership of Corporate Business
The Official is the majority shareholder of a closely held corporation (referred to as "Corporation"). The minority shareholders are the Official's children and a few of the corporation's employees. The Official therefore holds greater than a 10 percent interest in the entity.

(b) Sales Activities
Corporation engages in the sale of automotive merchandise, parts and supplies. Corporation operates five retail sales and service centers throughout Southern California as well as a major warehouse and mobile service units.

(c) Nature of Corporation’s Customers
Corporation does not engage in wholesale sales business, by which is meant the sale to dealers of automotive products for resale. Corporation’s customers consist of a diverse group, ranging from large commercial customers whose purchases may include parts, service and supplies for a fleet of trucks or automobiles to individual customers who acquire parts and supplies for their personal automobile in an over-the-counter transaction. During the most recent calendar year, Corporation’s sales activity shows dealings with nearly 4,000 separate customers. Of this number, 92% of Corporation’s sales volume involved commercial customers who maintain commercial accounts with Corporation and 8% represent cash or over the counter sales. Amongst Corporation’s commercial customers are several large customers who operate sizeable fleets and many smaller commercial customers who operate only a few vehicles.

(d) Customer Doing Business with Public Agency
Of the customers described in subsection 1(c), above, 11 are corporate entities who have periodically had contractual dealings with the public agency on which the Official serves. Corporation’s sales volume related to these 11 entities represented 2.22% of its total sales volume for its current year and has, in prior

years, constituted a slightly smaller percentage. The largest single customer amongst this group represented .0033 percent of gross sales revenue for the current year.

(e) Marketing Activities
The Official is not actively involved in sales activities for the Corporation and spends much of his time on public and charitable activities. The Corporation is managed primarily by a staff of long-time employees, including two of the Official's children.

2. Requested Assistance

Assistance is requested in applying the provisions of Government Code section 87103.5 or other pertinent provisions in making a determination as to conflict of interest when issues come before the agency in question related to the customers of Corporation described in subsection 1(d). In particular, does the business of Corporation constitute the retail sale of goods or services, as described in section 87103.5 and would that section be applicable to the circumstances described in this letter? Further, is the financial interest of the Official, as described, sufficiently material as to require his disqualification from participation in governmental decisions related to the customers or type of customer referred in subsection 1(d)?

Your review of these facts and advice concerning the application of the referenced code sections will be very much appreciated. Should you require additional information, please do not hesitate to contact the undersigned.

Very truly yours,

[Signature]
ROBERT R. JOHNSON

RRJ/jej
